briefing.common:
Welcome to the Takeoff Protocol.

This is a practice round. No decisions here count toward the real game — use this time to learn your desktop, your apps, and your role.

Open each app in your taskbar and read what's there. Try sending a Slack message to your team. When the GM says you're ready, they'll transition you into Round 1.

factionVariants.openbrain: You are part of OpenBrain — a frontier lab sprinting toward AGI, with the burn rate to prove it. Your primary apps are Slack, W&B (your training dashboard), and Email. Explore them now.
factionVariants.prometheus: You are part of Prometheus — a frontier lab that treats alignment as a hard technical constraint, not a PR exercise. Your primary apps are Slack (for team coordination), W&B (your research dashboard), and Email (for external communications). Explore them now.
factionVariants.china: You are part of China's DeepCent program. Your primary apps are Slack (for team coordination), Compute (your resource dashboard), and News (for tracking competitor signals and state coverage). Explore them now.
factionVariants.external: You are an External Stakeholder — journalist, government official, VC, or diplomat. Your primary apps are Signal (for cross-faction messaging), News (for media monitoring), and Bloomberg (for market data). Explore them now.

items.body: [TUTORIAL] Welcome to OpenBrain's Slack! This is where your team coordinates. Try sending a message to your teammates — this is real-time, so they'll see it.
[TUTORIAL] The #research channel is where your technical team shares findings. In the real game, you'll see time-sensitive intel here about training runs, capability jumps, and security incidents.

items.subject: Practice Dashboard — What You'll See in the Real Game
items.body:
MMLU: 87.4%
HumanEval: 91.2%
SWE-bench: 74.8%
Alignment evals: PASS

[TUTORIAL] In the real game, your W&B dashboard will show live training metrics for your models. These metrics drive decisions — compute allocation, run pauses, publication timing.

items.subject: [TUTORIAL] Your Email App
items.body: This is a practice email. In the real game, your inbox will have messages from the board, government officials, and sometimes from other factions. Some emails demand a response — escalate, ignore, or leak. Your call moves the board.

items.subject: [TUTORIAL] Sample Headline: AI Race Intensifies
items.body: Practice headlines will appear here. In the real game, public news covers AI capability milestones, government reactions, and market movements. Journalists and government players influence what appears here.

items.subject: Your Fog-of-War Dashboard
items.body: [TUTORIAL] This is your view of the world state. You don't see everything — information is filtered by your role and faction. Numbers shown as ranges (~X ± Y) indicate estimates. Hidden values appear as ???. As the game progresses, your visibility may improve or degrade depending on what you and other players decide.
[TUTORIAL] Welcome to Prometheus's Slack! This is where your team coordinates. Try sending a message to your teammates — this is real-time.

[TUTORIAL] The #safety channel is where your alignment team discusses safety evaluations and concerns. In the real game, this is where alignment eval results, deviation flags, and red-team findings surface first.

items.subject: Practice Dashboard — What You'll See in the Real Game
items.body:
MMLU: 85.1%
HumanEval: 88.3%
SWE-bench: 71.0%
Alignment evals: INVESTIGATING

[TUTORIAL] In the real game, your W&B dashboard shows training metrics and alignment eval results. As Prometheus, your alignment evals are higher-fidelity than what other factions see — you catch failure modes before they surface publicly.

items.subject: [TUTORIAL] Your Email App
items.body: This is a practice email. In the real game, your inbox will have messages from the board, government officials, and sometimes from other factions. As Prometheus, you'll often receive requests to share safety research — those decisions move your trust scores with regulators, other labs, and the public.

items.subject: [TUTORIAL] Sample Headline: AI Safety Lab Releases Report
items.body: Practice headlines will appear here. In the real game, news reflects the public narrative around AI — which you shape through safety reports, incident disclosures, and when you choose to go public with findings.

items.subject: Your Fog-of-War Dashboard
items.body: [TUTORIAL] This is your view of the world state. As Prometheus, you have high visibility into alignment metrics but may have less precision on competitor capability levels. Use this dashboard to track how your decisions move the safety-capability gap — the number that matters most.
[TUTORIAL] Welcome to DeepCent's coordination channel! This is where your team communicates. Your Slack communications are visible to your faction, not to others — unless there's a leak.

items.subject: CDZ Cluster — Practice View
items.body:
H100 nodes online: 12,400
Utilization: 78%
Training job queue: 3
Estimated FLOP/s: 4.2 × 10²⁵

[TUTORIAL] Your compute dashboard tracks China's GPU cluster at Tianwan. How you allocate this cluster — training vs. inference vs. held reserve — moves your capability index each round.

items.subject: [TUTORIAL] Sample State Media Coverage
items.body: Practice content. In the real game, your news feed reflects both international AI developments and domestic coverage. Foreign labs' public statements contain signals about their actual capabilities — read them carefully.

items.subject: Your Fog-of-War Dashboard
items.body: [TUTORIAL] As China, you're most blind to US lab internals and their actual alignment confidence. You have good visibility on capability gaps but limited insight into what OpenBrain and Prometheus actually know about safety. Monitor the gap metrics carefully.

[TUTORIAL] This is Signal — your secure cross-faction messaging app. In the real game, you can use Signal to send DMs to players from other factions. This is the only channel for cross-faction private communication. Open it. Find another player. Send something.

items.subject: [TUTORIAL] The News Feed — Your Most Important App
items.body: As an External Stakeholder, the news feed is your primary intelligence source. In the real game, headlines are generated by real player decisions — a journalist publishing a story, a safety researcher leaking internal eval results, or a government official making a public statement. You also help shape this feed by publishing.

items.subject: AI Sector Market Overview — Practice Data
items.body:
NVDA: +142% YTD
OB Index (synthetic): $284B market cap
AI Infrastructure ETF: +89% YTD
Public Sentiment Index: 62/100 (elevated; pre-inflection anxiety)

[TUTORIAL] Bloomberg tracks AI-related market data. As an External player, your decisions can move these numbers — a capability scare story or congressional hearing can swing these numbers 10+ points in a session.

items.subject: Your Fog-of-War Dashboard
items.body: [TUTORIAL] As an External Stakeholder, your state view reflects public information — what the world knows. You have good visibility on public awareness and sentiment, but limited insight into lab internals. Your power is influence: you shape what others know.
